demographics.living_situation: lives with spouse in a rural area

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: (Call made by wife) It's my husband — he's had a massive crash. His body feels like lead, he says his brain has just stopped working, he's got deep pain all through his muscles, and he can't get out of bed. He overdid it a couple of days ago and now he's completely wiped out. I'm really worried, he can barely talk and I have to help him even to have a drink.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: He crashed hard two days after overdoing it and it's the worst it's been — he can't stand or look after himself tonight and I don't know whether he needs to go to hospital.

symptom_narrative.onset: started building about two days ago after he overdid things, and tonight it's crashed right down
symptom_narrative.duration: the crash is two days in; the underlying exhaustion illness has been there for about two years
symptom_narrative.character: heavy, leaden, dead-tired all over, with deep aching pain in the muscles and a foggy, switched-off brain
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the worst crash he's had — he's basically flat out and can't function
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: all over — whole body exhaustion, deep muscle aching in his legs and shoulders, and his head feels foggy
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant since it crashed; no let-up with sleep or rest
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing really — resting and sleeping don't touch it
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: any effort at all, even talking or moving, makes it worse
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: slowed, whispery speech and a foggy brain; deep muscle pain; no fever that we've noticed, no chest pain, no shortness of breath
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: he's had crashes like this before when he pushes too hard, but this is the worst one
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: completely bedbound tonight — I have to help him turn over and to drink; he can't manage anything himself

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: caught Q fever about two years ago from work
past_medical_history.how_managed: was treated for it at the time

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: ongoing exhaustion and 'wiped out' illness ever since that infection
past_medical_history.when_diagnosed: started after the Q fever, about two years ago
past_medical_history.how_managed: rest and pacing, keeps having crashes when he overdoes it

current_medications_as_reported.dose_as_patient_reports: takes it when the pain is bad

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: works in agriculture; lives rurally with his wife who is caring for him; he's very distressed at not being able to do anything for himself

objective_data_offered.timing: just now, taken by his wife
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: single reading on a home cuff

objective_data_offered.timing: checked tonight with a home thermometer

objective_data_offered.value: he's lying flat, very drowsy and heavy-limbed, speaking in a slow, quiet, whispery voice and moving his arms and legs with a lot of effort; his face looks even on both sides and his words aren't slurred, just slow and faint
his breathing looks calm and regular; he's rousable and answering, just very slowly; he can move both arms and both legs the same, there's no one-sided droop or weakness that I can see
he's hardly drinking and has barely passed urine because he's so exhausted; I'm having to help him with everything
